'''Rowan Milnes''' (born 1 September 1999) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|so}} or {{rlp|sh}} for the [[Bradford Bulls]] in the [[Super League]].
 
He has previously played for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] and [[Castleford Tigers]] in the Super League, and for [[Bradford Bulls]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]] and [[RFL League 1|League 1]]. He has spent time on loan or dual registration at [[Wakefield Trinity]] and [[Salford Red Devils]] in the Super League, and at [[Dewsbury Rams]] in the Championship.

==Background==
Milnes was born in [[Bradford]], [[West Yorkshire]], England.{{citation needed|date=February 2020}}

He is a product of the [[Bradford Bulls Academy]] system. He signed a professional contract with the Bulls prior to the 2018 season.<ref name="Milnes Signs Professional Deal">{{cite web|url=http://www.bradfordbulls.co.uk/article/50608/five-talented-youngsters-commit-future-to-bradford-bulls|title=FIVE TALENTED YOUNGSTERS COMMIT FUTURE TO BRADFORD BULLS|publisher=Bradford Bulls|date=20 December 2017|accessdate=1 April 2019}}</ref>

== Playing career ==
=== Bradford Bulls ===
In the [[2018 Bradford Bulls season|2018 season]], Milnes featured in the pre-season friendlies against [[Halifax R.L.F.C.]], [[Sheffield Eagles]] and [[Dewsbury Rams]]. He scored against [[Halifax R.L.F.C.]] (1 goal) and [[Sheffield Eagles]] (2 goals). He played in Round 2 ([[London Skolars]]) then in Round 10 ([[Newcastle Thunder]]).<ref name="Milnes Makes Debut">{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/16218256.rowan-milnes-set-for-competitive-bradford-bulls-debut/|title=Rowan Milnes set for competitive Bradford Bulls debut|publisher=Telegraph and Argus|date=10 May 2018|accessdate=1 April 2019}}</ref>

In the [[2019 Bradford Bulls season|2019 season]], Milnes featured in the pre-season friendlies against [[York City Knights]], [[Dewsbury Rams]], [[Batley Bulldogs]] and [[Toronto Wolfpack]]. He scored against [[Dewsbury Rams]] (2 goals) and [[Batley Bulldogs]] (1 goal). Milnes played in Round 3 ([[Sheffield Eagles]]) then in Round 8 ([[Leigh Centurions]]) to Round 14 ([[Halifax R.L.F.C.]]). Milnes also featured in the [[2019 Challenge Cup]] in Round 4 ([[Keighley Cougars]]) to Quarter Final ([[Halifax R.L.F.C.]]). He scored against [[Leigh Centurions]] (1 goal), [[Dewsbury Rams]] (1 try), [[Halifax R.L.F.C.]] (1 try, 3 goals), [[Toronto Wolfpack]] (1 try) and [[Leeds Rhinos]] (4 goals).

Following the financial issues at the club, he signed for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] along with four other Bradford players, however Milnes was loaned back to the Bulls for the 2020 season.{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

===Hull Kingston Rovers===
On 12 August 2023, Milnes played for Hull Kingston Rovers in their 17-16 golden point extra-time loss to [[Leigh Leopards|Leigh]] in the Challenge Cup final.<ref>{{cite web |title=Challenge Cup final: Hull KR 16-17 Leigh Leopards - Leigh claim first Wembley final win in 52 years |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/66463438 |website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref> Milnes played a total of 18 games for Hull Kingston Rovers in the 2023 Super League season as the club finished fourth on the table and qualified for the playoffs.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/live/rugby-league/67008107|title=Relive Wigan's thumping win v Hull KR to head to Grand Final|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>

==== Bradford Bulls (loan) ====
In the [[2020 Bradford Bulls season|2020 season]], Milnes featured in the pre-season friendlies against [[Castleford Tigers]], [[Leeds Rhinos]], [[Dewsbury Rams]] and [[York City Knights]]. He scored against [[Castleford Tigers]] (1 try, 2 goals) and [[Dewsbury Rams]] (2 goals). Milnes played in Round 1 ([[London Broncos]]) to Round 5 ([[Sheffield Eagles]]). Rowan also featured in the [[2020 Challenge Cup]] in Round 4 ([[Underbank Rangers]]) to Round 5 ([[Wakefield Trinity]]). He scored against [[Featherstone Rovers]] (3 goals), [[Underbank Rangers]] (1 try, 3 goals), [[Oldham R.L.F.C.]] (5 goals), [[Sheffield Eagles]] (4 goals) and [[Wakefield Trinity]] (1 try, 3 goals).{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

=== Castleford Tigers ===
On 8 October 2023, the [[Castleford Tigers]] announced the signing of Milnes on a two-year deal from the [[2024 Castleford Tigers season|2024 season]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=8 October 2023 |title=Milnes joins the Tigers |url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=8786 |access-date=8 October 2023 |website=Castleford Tigers}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Olawumi |first=Ben |date=8 October 2023 |title=Castleford Tigers swoop in to sign back from fellow Super League outfit: 'It's a fresh start and I can't wait to get going' |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-swoop-in-to-sign-back-from-fellow-super-league-outfit-its-a-fresh-start-and-i-cant-wait-to-get-going |access-date=8 October 2023 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref> He suffered a partial dislocation of his shoulder in a pre-season friendly against [[London Broncos|London]] in January.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Shaw |first=Matthew |date=23 January 2024 |title=Castleford Tigers given Rowan Milnes injury option with two recovery routes open |url=https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers-rowan-milnes-injury-28495686 |access-date=25 February 2024 |website=Yorkshire Live}}</ref> Milnes made his Castleford debut in round 2 against the [[Salford Red Devils]],<ref>{{Cite web |date=25 February 2024 |title=Super League: Salford Red Devils 26-22 Castleford Tigers - Hosts hold on for first win |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/68337686 |access-date=25 February 2024 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> and scored his first try on 23 March against the [[Batley Bulldogs]] in the [[2024 Challenge Cup|Challenge Cup]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=23 March 2024 |title=Challenge Cup: Batley Bulldogs 14-28 Castleford Tigers: Paul McShane stars as Super League side progress |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/68616745 |access-date=24 March 2024 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> In round 10 against [[Leigh Leopards|Leigh]], Milnes successfully kicked two late touchline conversions to level the scores.<ref>{{Cite web |date=4 May 2024 |title=Leigh and Castleford draw in extra-time classic |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cx7dyw01q66o |access-date=5 May 2024 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> In round 16, he kicked two penalty goals to earn Castleford an away victory against [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] for only the second time in 33 years.<ref>{{Cite web |date=5 July 2024 |title=Castleford stun St Helens to end losing run |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cw0yjy8jz80o |access-date=7 July 2024 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Bower |first=Aaron |date=6 July 2024 |title=Castleford Tigers' incredible Jekyll and Hyde record at St Helens dissected after famous victory |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-incredible-jekyll-and-hyde-record-at-st-helens-dissected-after-famous-victory |access-date=7 July 2024 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref>

==== Hull Kingston Rovers (loan) ====
On 6 August 2025, Milnes rejoined [[Hull KR]] on an initial two-week loan from Castleford.<ref>{{Cite web |date=6 August 2025 |title=Castleford's Milnes returns to Hull KR on loan |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cn43wn9y90po |access-date=6 August 2025 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> He provided cover at half-back with both [[Tyrone May]] and [[Danny Richardson (rugby league)|Danny Richardson]] unavailable, although did not register an appearance in his two weeks at the Robins.<ref name="BBC HKR Sal loan">{{Cite web |date=22 August 2025 |title=Salford sign half-back Milnes on loan as two more depart |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cn5epwq3lezo |access-date=15 October 2025 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref>

=== Return to Hull Kingston Rovers ===
On 22 August 2025, Milnes was released from the remainder of his contract at Castleford and subsequently signed for Hull Kingston Rovers for the rest of the [[2025 Hull Kingston Rovers season|2025 season]].<ref name="BBC HKR Sal loan" />

====Salford Red Devils (loan)====
Upon signing for Hull KR, Milnes went out on loan to [[Salford Red Devils]] with immediate effect.<ref name="BBC HKR Sal loan" /><ref>{{cite web|website=Salford Red Devils|date=22 Aug 2025|accessdate=30 Aug 2025|author=Harry Siddall|title=ROWAN MILNES JOINS THE REDS ON LOAN|url=https://www.salfordreddevils.com/rowan-milnes-joins-the-reds-on-loan/}}</ref> He featured in the final five rounds of the season for the struggling Salford, including their shock round 24 win over [[Warrington Wolves]] in which he kicked four conversions and one drop-goal.<ref>{{Cite web |date=29 August 2025 |title=Salford stun Warrington to secure rare win |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cd6nd5v3v4vo |access-date=19 October 2025 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=30 August 2025 |title=Super League: Salford Red Devils claim shock victory at Warrington Wolves despite turmoil as Hull KR beat St Helens |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12204/13420723/super-league-salford-red-devils-claim-shock-victory-at-warrington-wolves-in-spite-of-off-field-turmoil |access-date=19 October 2025 |website=Sky Sports}}</ref>

===Return to Bradford Bulls===
On 17 October 2025, one day after their promotion to [[Super League]] was confirmed, [[Bradford Bulls]] announced the signing of Milnes on a two-year deal.<ref>{{cite web |author=Chapman Coombe |first=Louis |date=17 October 2025 |title=Bradford Bulls make first Super League signing as ex-Hull KR star joins |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/bradford-bulls-make-first-super-league-signing-as-ex-hull-kr-star-joins |access-date=19 October 2025 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Atkinson |first=Nathan |date=17 October 2025 |title=Bradford Bulls get Rowan Milnes after Super League spells |url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportlatest/25550250.bradford-bulls-get-rowan-milnes-back-castleford-tigers/ |access-date=19 October 2025 |website=The Telegraph and Argus}}</ref>
